title: Review: relay ws/store handlers, wallet scanner/tx_builder, core messages round 25
notes:
Confidence: 25% chance of missed bug. Uncertainty driven by: (1) not having traced full payment flow end-to-end (payment.rs calling next_diversifier standalone is suspect), (2) scanner state machine has complex snapshot/restore logic with 4 unrestored paths now identified, (3) scan_tx witness ordering correctness depends on strict Update-before-Append ordering that is correct but relies on iteration order over self.witnesses.iter_mut() which is insertion-order in HashMap — not guaranteed, but inconsequential since all witnesses are updated identically. Would raise confidence with: integration tests that run the scanner against a replay of real mainnet compact blocks, end-to-end tests for GROUP_LEAVE/GROUP_ADD, and mutation testing on the key package cap logic.
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — The three correctness bugs (scan_block snapshot gaps, GROUP_LEAVE offline membership guard, key package refresh at cap) are all latent and require specific triggers. None are exploitable for remote code execution, but scan_block could cause permanent note loss which is irreversible without a rescan. The GROUP_LEAVE bug is annoying but not data-corrupting. The key package cap bug affects legitimate multi-device users.
